question: A forwarder on web02 sends WinEventLog:Security data to index=security, where the events are stored and made searchable. The task involves a panel backed by a saved search. An analyst asks which Splunk component stores indexed data and processes many search requests. Which component is the best answer?
choices: Lookup editor
Dashboard panel
Time picker
Indexer

evidenceArtifacts.title: Deployment observation
rows: Run SPL and inspect results in Splunk Web

explanation: Why this is right: The indexer parses and stores incoming data in indexes and participates in searches over that data. Search heads coordinate user searches, while dashboards and time pickers are interface features rather than indexing components. Why the alternatives are wrong: The other options assign the task to a component or interface object that does not own the described responsibility. Review takeaway: Choose the component or user feature that owns the described responsibility; interface objects do not perform indexing.

question: A user opens Splunk Web on the search tier, enters SPL, and receives results from index=network. The task involves results handed off by the previous analyst. A user opens Splunk Web, enters SPL, and views results. Which component provides the primary search interface?
choices: Index bucket
Lookup file
Search head
Forwarder

evidenceArtifacts.title: Deployment observation
rows: Run SPL and inspect results in Splunk Web

explanation: Why this is right: A search head gives users the search interface and coordinates searches across indexed data. Forwarders collect and send data, index buckets store indexed data, and lookup files enrich events. Why the alternatives are wrong: The other options assign the task to a component or interface object that does not own the described responsibility. Review takeaway: Choose the component or user feature that owns the described responsibility; interface objects do not perform indexing.

question: A security workspace must distribute related searches, reports, dashboards, and navigation as one unit. The task involves a search being prepared for reuse. A team installs a Splunk app for security monitoring. What does a Splunk app generally provide?
choices: A Splunk app is a shortcut to one saved search
A Splunk app packages knowledge objects, dashboards, views, and configuration for a purpose or audience
A Splunk app is one uploaded data source and its source type
An app is the same thing as one scheduled alert

evidenceArtifacts.title: Deployment observation
rows: Run SPL and inspect results in Splunk Web

explanation: Why this is right: Splunk apps organize related knowledge objects, dashboards, reports, views, and configuration. They are not simply one alert, one data file, or a browser shortcut. Why the alternatives are wrong: The other options assign the task to a component or interface object that does not own the described responsibility. Review takeaway: Choose the component or user feature that owns the described responsibility; interface objects do not perform indexing.

question: An analyst needs a different time zone and default app without changing roles, source types, or indexed data. The task involves an event set with unrelated matches. Which TWO statements correctly apply to this splunk basics workflow?

evidenceArtifacts.title: Deployment observation
rows: Run SPL and inspect results in Splunk Web

rows: Run SPL and inspect results in Splunk Web

question: The current all-index search mixes Linux authentication events with data from index=main; the required events use sourcetype=linux_secure and user=root. The task involves an event list that needs refinement. An analyst wants the most efficient basic search for Linux authentication events. Which search best narrows by index, sourcetype, and field value?
choices: index=* user=*
index=auth sourcetype=linux_secure user=root
user=root
sourcetype=* root
explanation: Why this is right: A targeted search such as index=auth sourcetype=linux_secure user=root limits the data Splunk must scan and keeps the intent clear. Searches that omit index or sourcetype are broader and usually less efficient. Why the alternatives are wrong: The other options leave the search unnecessarily broad, change presentation before selection, or do not control the requested time and data scope. Review takeaway: Start with time, index=auth, and sourcetype=linux_secure constraints before adding presentation commands.

question: An all-time search returns older events, but the investigation only covers -30m@m to now. The task involves the fields sidebar and event details. A user gets too many historical events. What is the best way to restrict the search to the desired time period?
choices: Use the time picker or earliest/latest modifiers to limit the search window
Add fields * to the beginning of every search
Use rename before the base search terms
Save the search before choosing a time range
explanation: Why this is right: Splunk searches are strongly affected by time range. The time picker or earliest/latest modifiers limit the events scanned and returned, which improves relevance and performance. Why the alternatives are wrong: The other options leave the search unnecessarily broad, change presentation before selection, or do not control the requested time and data scope. Review takeaway: Start with time, index=sales, and sourcetype=csv constraints before adding presentation commands.

question: The Search view shows raw matching records with _time, host=web02, and extracted field host. The task involves a chart requested by an operations lead. A learner opens search results and reviews the event area. What does the events list represent?
choices: The events list displays saved-object metadata instead of matching events
The events list changes the license volume setting
The events list stores lookup definitions
The events list shows matching raw events and selected fields from the search results
explanation: Why this is right: The events list contains raw matching events and extracted or selected fields from the current search. It does not manage licensing, dashboards, or lookup definitions. Why the alternatives are wrong: The other options leave the search unnecessarily broad, change presentation before selection, or do not control the requested time and data scope. Review takeaway: Start with time, index=main, and sourcetype=syslog constraints before adding presentation commands.

question: A broad search returns 719 events from both index=web and index=network, including unrelated source types. The task involves a recurring compliance summary. Which TWO statements correctly apply to this basic searching workflow?
choices: Pause, resume, finalize, or inspect the search job from job controls
Remove the index and sourcetype constraints to widen the search
Use table before any search terms are entered
Use more specific search terms, fields, indexes, source types, or time ranges
Remove the time range to include all data
explanation: Why this is right: Refining a search means narrowing it with relevant terms, fields, index and sourcetype constraints, and a useful time range. Broadening the search usually makes the problem worse. The second correct statement is also required: Splunk lets users manage search jobs with actions such as pause, resume, finalize, and inspect depending on permissions and job state. These are job controls, not data model or source type administration. Why the alternatives are wrong: The other options leave the search unnecessarily broad, change presentation before selection, or do not control the requested time and data scope. Review takeaway: Start with time, index=web, and sourcetype=access_combined constraints before adding presentation commands.

question: The event histogram contains a sharp spike from host=fw-edge-01 within Last 60 minutes. The task involves an app-specific search workspace. A spike appears in search results. How does the timeline help?
choices: The timeline replaces field extraction settings
The timeline helps identify event distribution over time and select dense or interesting periods
The timeline creates lookup files automatically
The timeline controls retention for events outside the visible range
explanation: Why this is right: The timeline visualizes event counts across the selected time range. It helps users spot spikes, gaps, and periods worth drilling into. Why the alternatives are wrong: The other options leave the search unnecessarily broad, change presentation before selection, or do not control the requested time and data scope. Review takeaway: Start with time, index=security, and sourcetype=WinEventLog:Security constraints before adding presentation commands.

Change the index bucket size from the event table
Convert the job into a data model by default
Disable source types from the timeline

Use table before any search terms are entered

question: A long-running search is still scanning events after the analyst has enough evidence to stop or inspect the job. The task involves an event set with unrelated matches. A long-running search needs to be controlled. Which action belongs to search job controls?

Change the index bucket size from the event table
Convert the job into a data model by default
Disable source types from the timeline

question: One event contains host=app01, sourcetype=access_combined, and status=500. The task involves an app-specific search workspace. A learner asks what a field is in Splunk. Which answer is most accurate?
choices: Fields are searchable name-value pairs extracted from events
Fields are always separate indexes
Fields are labels that exist only inside dashboard panels
Fields are separate files imported beside the matching events
explanation: Why this is right: Fields are name-value pairs extracted from event data, such as host, source, sourcetype, status, or user. They make searches, filters, statistics, and reports more precise. Why the alternatives are wrong: The other options confuse field filtering with display changes or omit the field-value relationship required by the event evidence. Review takeaway: Treat status as a searchable name-value pair and verify that its value exists in the current result set.

question: The analyst must keep only events whose EventCode value is 4625. The task involves a report shared with another team. An analyst wants to filter events where EventCode equals 4625. Which field expression is correct?
choices: EventCode | 4625
field(4625)
rename EventCode as 4625
EventCode=4625
explanation: Why this is right: The expression EventCode=4625 filters events to those with that field value. Pipe commands such as rename are used after matching events, not as the basic equality expression. Why the alternatives are wrong: The other options confuse field filtering with display changes or omit the field-value relationship required by the event evidence. Review takeaway: Treat EventCode as a searchable name-value pair and verify that its value exists in the current result set.

The fields sidebar creates indexes automatically

Why the alternatives are wrong: The other options confuse field filtering with display changes or omit the field-value relationship required by the event evidence. Review takeaway: Treat action as a searchable name-value pair and verify that its value exists in the current result set.

Review takeaway: Treat user as a searchable name-value pair and verify that its value exists in the current result set.

question: The SPL starts with `index=web` and then sends those events to `table _time host status`. The task involves an HTTP error trend. A learner reviews SPL command chaining. What does the pipe character do?
choices: The pipe character changes the selected app
The pipe character starts a new index bucket
The pipe character sends the results of one command into the next command
The pipe starts a separate search that ignores the previous command output
explanation: Why this is right: In SPL, the pipe passes current search results to the next command in the pipeline. question: A validated search for Linux authentication events must be reused by the same team next week. The task involves an event set with unrelated matches. A recurring query should be reused and shared with a team. What should the user do first?
choices: Rename a result field without saving the search as a report
Save the search as a report
Create a new source type for the saved search output
Change the app navigation without saving the search as a knowledge object
explanation: Why this is right: Saving a search as a report creates a reusable knowledge object. Reports can then be edited, scheduled, shared, or placed on dashboards depending on permissions. Why the alternatives are wrong: The other options alter unrelated data or administrative settings instead of saving or presenting the validated search. Review takeaway: Validate the underlying search first, then save and present it without implying that the raw indexed data changes.

Review takeaway: Validate the underlying search first, then save and present it without implying that the raw indexed data changes.

question: An operations page must present related panels for system messages, host health, and volume. The task involves field filters being checked for accuracy. A manager wants one page with several operational charts. What Splunk object fits?

Review takeaway: Validate the underlying search first, then save and present it without implying that the raw indexed data changes.

question: Events contain user=root; a CSV maps that key to owner, department, and priority. The task involves results handed off by the previous analyst. A CSV maps user IDs to departments. Why would a Splunk user create a lookup?
choices: Lookups erase raw event text after a search
Lookups replace the time picker
Lookups only rename columns in a dashboard result table
Lookups enrich events by adding fields from an external table or defined lookup source
explanation: Why this is right: Lookups add context to events by matching event fields to rows in a lookup source and returning additional fields. They are commonly used for enrichment. Why the alternatives are wrong: The other options do not join event fields to an external key and therefore cannot return the requested enrichment. Review takeaway: Successful enrichment requires a matching key such as user and explicitly returned lookup fields.

question: A CSV has been uploaded, but searches still need a named configuration that describes how to use it. The task involves a search being prepared for reuse. A CSV lookup file has been uploaded. What is the purpose of the lookup definition?
choices: A lookup definition stores visualization settings for a dashboard
A lookup definition controls retention for the lookup file
A lookup definition tells Splunk how to use the lookup file or lookup source in searches
A lookup definition is the same as an index bucket
explanation: Why this is right: A lookup definition configures how Splunk refers to and uses the lookup source. Users can then call the lookup from SPL or configure automatic lookups where appropriate. Why the alternatives are wrong: The other options do not join event fields to an external key and therefore cannot return the requested enrichment. Review takeaway: Successful enrichment requires a matching key such as region and explicitly returned lookup fields.

question: The event field host matches a CSV key that can return owner and severity. The task involves an event set with unrelated matches. A team wants to enrich events that contain host. Which lookup use is most appropriate?
choices: Use top to create the lookup definition automatically
Use a lookup keyed on host to add descriptive fields such as owner, department, or severity
Use rename host as owner to import all lookup rows
Use sort to upload the CSV file
explanation: Why this is right: A lookup can match an event field such as host to an external table and add related fields. Commands like rename, sort, and top do not import or define lookup enrichment by themselves. Why the alternatives are wrong: The other options do not join event fields to an external key and therefore cannot return the requested enrichment. Review takeaway: Successful enrichment requires a matching key such as host and explicitly returned lookup fields.

question: A lookup returns no enrichment for status=500, even though both the events and CSV should share that key. The task involves a reusable operational view. Which TWO statements correctly apply to this creating and using lookups workflow?

Review takeaway: Successful enrichment requires a matching key such as status and explicitly returned lookup fields.

question: A compliance search must execute automatically every Monday without a user opening Splunk Web. The task involves a panel backed by a saved search. A weekly compliance report should run automatically. What does scheduling a report do?
choices: A scheduled report refreshes the lookup file instead of executing its saved search
A scheduled report replaces event indexing
A scheduled report runs a saved search on a defined schedule
A scheduled report changes the user default time range rather than running automatically
explanation: Why this is right: A scheduled report executes a saved search at configured times. It helps automate recurring reporting without requiring a user to run the search manually each time. Why the alternatives are wrong: The other options do not combine a tested search with the schedule, threshold, and action required by the workflow. Review takeaway: A reliable scheduled object combines a tested search, an appropriate time window, and a meaningful schedule or trigger.

question: The team needs a notification only when a search for failed Windows logons crosses an operational threshold. The task involves results handed off by the previous analyst. A team wants notification when failed logons exceed a threshold. Which Splunk feature fits?
choices: An alert is required before every ad hoc search
An alert is used when search results meet a defined condition that should trigger an action or notification
An alert is a dashboard visualization state without a search condition
An alert is a raw data source type
explanation: Why this is right: Alerts monitor search results for defined conditions and can trigger actions such as notifications. They are appropriate when a result should prompt attention. Why the alternatives are wrong: The other options do not combine a tested search with the schedule, threshold, and action required by the workflow. Review takeaway: A reliable scheduled object combines a tested search, an appropriate time window, and a meaningful schedule or trigger.

question: A saved search should run every five minutes and test its result count even when no dashboard is open. The task involves a search being prepared for reuse. A learner reviews scheduled alert behavior. Which statement is accurate?
choices: Scheduled alerts evaluate results at intervals and trigger when the configured condition is met
A scheduled alert requires an active Splunk Web session to evaluate
Scheduled alerts require raw events to be manually pasted into Splunk
Scheduled alerts permanently disable the search head
explanation: Why this is right: Scheduled alerts run searches on a schedule and evaluate trigger conditions. They do not require a dashboard view to be open at the moment they run. Why the alternatives are wrong: The other options do not combine a tested search with the schedule, threshold, and action required by the workflow. Review takeaway: A reliable scheduled object combines a tested search, an appropriate time window, and a meaningful schedule or trigger.

Choose an alert condition that matches the operational risk, such as result count greater than a threshold
An alert is a raw data source type
An alert is required before every ad hoc search
An alert is used when search results meet a defined condition that should trigger an action or notification
explanation: Why this is right: Alerts monitor search results for defined conditions and can trigger actions such as notifications. They are appropriate when a result should prompt attention. The second correct statement is also required: Alert quality depends on a meaningful search, time window, and trigger condition. Thresholds and result conditions should reflect the risk the team actually wants to catch. Why the alternatives are wrong: The other options do not combine a tested search with the schedule, threshold, and action required by the workflow. Review takeaway: A reliable scheduled object combines a tested search, an appropriate time window, and a meaningful schedule or trigger.

question: A saved search should run every five minutes and test its result count even when no dashboard is open. The task involves a recurring compliance summary. A learner reviews scheduled alert behavior. Which statement is accurate?
choices: Scheduled alerts evaluate results at intervals and trigger when the configured condition is met
A scheduled alert requires an active Splunk Web session to evaluate
Scheduled alerts require raw events to be manually pasted into Splunk
Scheduled alerts permanently disable the search head
explanation: Why this is right: Scheduled alerts run searches on a schedule and evaluate trigger conditions. They do not require a dashboard view to be open at the moment they run. Why the alternatives are wrong: The other options do not combine a tested search with the schedule, threshold, and action required by the workflow. Review takeaway: A reliable scheduled object combines a tested search, an appropriate time window, and a meaningful schedule or trigger.

question: The current alert fires for every result, but action is only warranted when more than 40 events occur in Last 60 minutes. The task involves an app-specific search workspace. A noisy alert fires too often. What should be reviewed first?
choices: Trigger every alert for every event regardless of severity
Use the visualization title as the alert trigger
Avoid time windows because alerts do not use time
Choose an alert condition that matches the operational risk, such as result count greater than a threshold
explanation: Why this is right: Alert quality depends on a meaningful search, time window, and trigger condition. Thresholds and result conditions should reflect the risk the team actually wants to catch. Why the alternatives are wrong: The other options do not combine a tested search with the schedule, threshold, and action required by the workflow. Review takeaway: A reliable scheduled object combines a tested search, an appropriate time window, and a meaningful schedule or trigger.

question: A compliance search must execute automatically every Monday without a user opening Splunk Web. The task involves a report shared with another team. A weekly compliance report should run automatically. What does scheduling a report do?
choices: A scheduled report refreshes the lookup file instead of executing its saved search
A scheduled report replaces event indexing
A scheduled report runs a saved search on a defined schedule
A scheduled report changes the user default time range rather than running automatically
explanation: Why this is right: A scheduled report executes a saved search at configured times. It helps automate recurring reporting without requiring a user to run the search manually each time. Why the alternatives are wrong: The other options do not combine a tested search with the schedule, threshold, and action required by the workflow. Review takeaway: A reliable scheduled object combines a tested search, an appropriate time window, and a meaningful schedule or trigger.
